subject: Home Management
aims_and_objectives: Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of management concepts; goals, values, standards and needs
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of decision-making processes
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of human and material resources available for managing a home
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of basic skills needed in caring for the home and society
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of consumer skills
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of sound health practices
Assess knowledge, understanding, appreciation and application of self-reliant skills for effective living

scheme_of_examination.description: There will be three papers, Papers 1, 2, and 3 all of which must be taken. Papers 1 and 2 will be a composite paper to be taken at one sitting.
scheme_of_examination.paper_1: Comprise fifty multiple choice objective questions, all of which are to be answered in 1 hour for 50 marks.
scheme_of_examination.paper_2: Comprise six essay questions out of which candidates will be required to answer four within 1 hour 15 minutes for 50 marks.
scheme_of_examination.paper_3: A 3-hour practical test conducted by a visiting examiner appointed by WAEC, carrying 100 marks.

principle_of_management.contents: Definition and fundamental concepts of home management
Explore potential career paths and opportunities related to home management
(a) Planning, (b) Organization, (c) Implementation, (d) Evaluation. Application of process to specific tasks.
(a) Personal characteristics, (b) Values, (c) Goals, (d) Standards, (e) Needs, (f) Events
(a) Definition, (b) Characteristics, (c) Kinds - Human (time, energy, skill, work simplification as it applies to the home), Non-human/material community

principle_of_management.topic: Decision making & Management of time and energy
principle_of_management.contents: Decision-making processes, management of time, energy, and work simplification in the home

family_living.contents: (a) Definition, (b) Types - nuclear, monogamous, polygamous, extended, (c) Advantages and disadvantages of each type, (d) Family life cycle - beginning, expanding, contracting and empty-nest, (e) Functions of the family, (f) Roles and responsibilities of family members (mother, father, children, working mothers, single parent, etc.), (g) Family relationships, factors affecting family relationships, effects on well-being, conflict and conflict resolution

(a) Types of marriage, Boy/Girl relationship (definition, traditional/custom, guidelines for healthy relationship, problems of unhealthy relationship, consequences of indiscriminate sexual indulgence, sex education), (b) Choosing a life partner (factors to consider e.g. blood group, genotype, HIV status, dating, courtship, engagement), (c) Preparation for marriage (readiness - where to live, maturity, finances, introduction to each other's family), (d) Marriage Ceremony in different localities

(a) Preparation for parenthood (planning a family, reproduction, pregnancy, ante-natal care), (b) Child birth (labour, delivery, post-natal care - immunization), (c) Parenting (meaning, importance, responsibilities, provision of needs, bathing)

feeding_the_family.topic: Classes of food and food nutrients

feeding_the_family.contents: Basic cooking methods and culinary terminology
How cooking heat alters and affects food nutrients
Nutritional needs of infants, toddlers, adolescents, adults, pregnant women, lactating women, the aged, convalescents, invalids, and vegetarians
Methods and importance of storing and preserving food items
Planning of balanced meals for different groups of people
Kitchen layout, hygiene practices, and safety measures
Guidelines and practices for buying food economically and safely

feeding_the_family.topic: Preparation and serving of meals, snacks and beverages
feeding_the_family.contents: Cooking equipment, utensils, tableware, table setting, and service
Hosting, entertaining, and social etiquette in the home

clothing_the_family.contents: Meaning, types, and functions of clothing
Types and functions of household linen
Origin of fibres and classification of fibres/fabrics
Selecting and organizing clothing for individual and family needs

clothing_the_family.topic: Maintenance of clothing and laundry work
clothing_the_family.contents: Laundry agents (water, detergents, fabric rinses, stiffening agents, bleaches), laundry equipment and tools, management of the family wash, stains and stain removal, care of special garments
Tools usage and simple garment repairs

housing_the_family.contents: Definition of house, types of house (storey building, bungalow, duplex, etc.), classification of houses (traditional and modern)

(a) Acquiring a house (renting, mortgage, building, buying, inheritance, gift), (b) Factors influencing the choice of a house (economic, social, psychological, sites, ventilation, design), (c) Guidelines for choosing a family house
(a) Definition, difference between a home and a house, (b) Equipment and furnishing in the home (large/small equipment, hard/soft furnishing, fixtures)
(a) Meaning and importance, (b) Elements and principles of art and design (colours, textures, lines, proportion, rhythm, etc.), (c) Guidelines for interior decoration, (d) Flower arrangements

(a) Water (sources, uses, purification, storage), (b) Light (electricity meter reading, electric bill), (c) Fuels (uses, storage, control, advantages, disadvantages of cooking gas, kerosene, firewood, charcoal, electricity)

(a) Cleaning agents and materials (water, soaps, abrasives, polishes), (b) Cleaning equipment and tools, (c) Care of surfaces (wood, tile, formica, concrete, plastic, etc.), (d) Care of floors/floor coverings/finishing (carpets, linoleums, mats, rugs, terrazzo, parquet, etc.), (e) Care of home furnishing and ornaments, (f) Cleaning functional areas, (g) Bed making
(a) Importance, (b) Common accidents, (c) Prevention, (d) Use of first aid
(a) Definition, (b) Importance of sanitation, (c) Waste disposal (household waste, drainage system, disposal methods, pollution and health hazards), (d) Household pests (types, control)

wealth_creation_and_management.contents: (a) Meaning, (b) Importance, (c) Relationship between creativity and entrepreneurship, (d) Entrepreneurial skills (creativity, management, communication), (e) Entrepreneurial activities/enterprises (fashion designing, hair dressing, fast foods, bead making, etc.)
(a) Meaning, (b) Guidelines, (c) Processes, (d) Family budget (income, expenditure, steps in budgeting)
(a) Meaning, (b) Types, (c) Advantages, (d) Ways of creating wealth (saving, investment, trading, etc.)
(a) Meaning, (b) Advantages, (c) Guidelines, (d) Investment opportunities (stocks, shares, treasury bills)

consumer_education.topic: Rights and responsibilities of a consumer

consumer_education.contents: (i) Market and market survey (meaning, types, uses/importance, procedures), (ii) Wise purchasing practices, (iii) Prevention of wastage in the home
Effective and wise market purchasing techniques

reading_texts: Home Management by R.O. Johnson, A. Akingbehin and J. Mifie. Publisher: Longman
Home Management for Secondary Schools by Enid O'Reilly Wright & Lillian Adesimi-Davies. Publisher: Evans Brothers Ltd.
Home Management for Secondary Schools 1-3 by Mary Edna Aufani Joe, Oby Eyisi et al. Publisher: University Press Plc.
Home Management for Secondary Schools 1005 Q & A Series by Elizabeth Anyakoha. Publisher: Fep Publisher Ltd.
